steps.title: Support a net increase
steps.content: The researchers conclude that net CO2 is likely to increase if warming hastens spring snow melt. The passage presents two possible directions: plants can absorb more CO2 through productivity, while soil microorganisms can release more CO2 through heterotrophic respiration.

steps.title: Choose effects pointing upward
steps.content: Choice A is mixed because slower plant growth would reduce absorption, but suppressed respiration would also reduce output. Choice B points against the conclusion by increasing plant absorption. Choice C is too vague because amplified effects could move either way. A finding that lowers plant growth and raises respiration makes net CO2 increase more likely. The answer is D.
